terms.definition: Similarity function K(x, x′) that equals an inner product ⟨φ(x), φ(x′)⟩ in some feature space. Canonical definition in this topic.
Replace explicit dot products in an algorithm with K(x, x′) so it operates in an implicit φ-space without computing φ(x). Canonical definition in this topic.
Mapping from input x to a (possibly high- or infinite-dimensional) representation φ(x) such that K(x, x′) = ⟨φ(x), φ(x′)⟩.
A φ that is never materialized as vectors; only pairwise kernel values K(x, x′) are computed. Canonical definition in this topic.
Matrix G with entries Gᵢⱼ = K(xᵢ, xⱼ) for a finite set of inputs; must be positive semi-definite for valid kernels.
K(x, x′) = exp(−γ ‖x − x′‖²); similarity decays with squared Euclidean distance; γ controls locality. Canonical definition in this topic.
K(x, x′) = (γ x · x′ + r)^d; encodes feature interactions up to degree d without explicit expansion. Canonical definition in this topic.
Requirement that K produce a positive semi-definite Gram matrix for any finite input set — necessary for K to correspond to some φ.
